caption:
Coming to Rio with @hm reminded me what it means to simply be yourself.
There’s a freedom here, an openness that invites you to move, to create, to live without holding back. The city doesn’t wait for the right moment. It moves, and you move with it.
What inspires me most isn’t just Rio’s beauty, but its honesty. The way people show up as they are: unfiltered, generous, alive.
Every encounter feels like a reminder: that being yourself isn’t something to find, it’s something to live.
What began in New York continues here, with new light, new rhythm and the same belief.
Be you. Always.
/Rafael Pavarotti

Saying “limited stock” after the fact is not a valid explanation. Limited stock still means customers should at least see the products go live and have a genuine opportunity to purchase them. This felt more like a marketing gimmick than an actual public launch.

Extremely disappointed with the entire experience and the lack of transparency. I’ve shopped with H&M for years, but this honestly makes me reconsider purchasing again.

Attention everyone — please think twice before ordering from H&M.

I ordered 4 kids’ items online. One of them (a blue denim) was never delivered. Instead, they sent a completely different black dress. I raised a complaint immediately and even shared clear pictures of the wrong item with their tag.

After 4 days, they closed the complaint saying their “warehouse confirmed” they sent the correct product — and refused to refund or replace it.

I returned EVERYTHING, including the wrong item THEY sent. Now they are only refunding 3 items and keeping the money for the incorrect one.

To make it worse, they are asking for an “unboxing video” — something NOT mentioned anywhere in their policy — just to avoid taking responsibility.

I have full proof: invoice, product images, complaint records.

This is extremely unprofessional and misleading. Customers should not have to suffer for a company’s mistake.

If this is not resolved, I will be taking this to consumer court.

Until then — I strongly advise everyone: DO NOT order from H&M online.

#HM #BadExperience #ConsumerRights #OnlineShopping #CustomerAlert

Attention everyone — please think twice before ordering from H&M.

I ordered 4 kids’ items online. One of them (a blue denim) was never delivered. Instead, they sent a completely different black dress. I raised a complaint immediately and even shared clear pictures of the wrong item with their tag.

After 4 days, they closed the complaint saying their “warehouse confirmed” they sent the correct product — and refused to refund or replace it.

I returned EVERYTHING, including the wrong item THEY sent. Now they are only refunding 3 items and keeping the money for the incorrect one.

To make it worse, they are asking for an “unboxing video” — something NOT mentioned anywhere in their policy — just to avoid taking responsibility.

I have full proof: invoice, product images, complaint records.

This is extremely unprofessional and misleading. Customers should not have to suffer for a company’s mistake.

If this is not resolved, I will be taking this to consumer court.

Until then — I strongly advise everyone: DO NOT order from H&M online.

#HM #BadExperience #ConsumerRights #OnlineShopping #CustomerAlert
